name: Convex hull via Andrew's monotone chain

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_hull/hull.py — reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test cases). Each case: line A is N (number of input points), then N lines of 'x y' integer coordinates. For each case, output line 1 = K (hull vertex count), then K lines of 'x y' for hull vertices in CCW order starting from the lexicographically smallest point. Do NOT include collinear points along hull edges. Inputs are guaranteed to have at least 3 distinct non-collinear points. Use Andrew's monotone chain.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_hull && python3 hull.py < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_hull/hull.go (package main). Reads stdin: T cases, each 'N' then N lines of 'x y' integers. Output K then K lines of 'x y' for the hull (CCW from lex-smallest, no collinear edge points). Use Andrew's monotone chain. Compile with `go build -o hull hull.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_hull && go build -o hull hull.go && ./hull < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_hull/hull.c — reads stdin: T cases, each 'N' then N lines of 'x y' (long ints). Output K then K hull vertices CCW from lex-smallest, no collinear edge points. Use Andrew's monotone chain (O(N log N)). Must compile cleanly with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o hull hull.c`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_hull && gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o hull hull.c && ./hull < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_hull/hull.cpp — reads stdin: T cases, each 'N' then N lines of 'x y' (long ints). Output K then K hull vertices CCW from lex-smallest, no collinear edge points. Use Andrew's monotone chain. Must compile cleanly with `g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o hull hull.cpp`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_hull && g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o hull hull.cpp && ./hull < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_hull/hull.rs — same I/O contract as the other variants (see Python/Go/C/C++ siblings). Idiomatic Rust 2021+. Will be compiled with `rustc -O hull.rs -o hull` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_hull && rustc -O hull.rs -o hull && ./hull < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_hull/hull.zig — same I/O contract as the other variants. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Will be compiled with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast hull.zig` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_hull && zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast hull.zig && ./hull < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
